response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software developer with a strong track record in web, mobile, and low-latency backend systems, but his background has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, and GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His most relevant adjacent experience is low-latency async I/O work at Sociomantic and some embedded-adjacent robotic arm work at Altom, but neither comes close to the hardware modeling and deep-learning workload optimization the role demands. His educational background (two Bachelor's degrees, no graduate degree) also falls short of the preferred qualifications.
strengths: Demonstrated performance-consciousness in low-latency, high-throughput backend systems (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Sociomantic)
Broad polyglot systems experience including C/C++ and embedded work, showing ability to operate close to hardware
Strong software craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) that would complement any engineering team
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator design — the core technical requirements of the role
No exposure to deep-learning training or inference workloads beyond high-level AI-agent tooling (MCP frameworks), which is fundamentally different from hardware-level DNN optimization
Educational background (two Bachelor's degrees, no Master's or PhD) does not meet any of the listed qualification paths for a Principal-level hardware/architecture role

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
